title: Memoir Is a Form of Drag
description:
Why is the act of painting one’s face so charged with gendered implications? How might makeup smear the lines drawn between “feminine” and “masculine”? And what can drag culture teach all of us about the role of artifice—and art—in constructing identity? Although our books differ in genre and scope, they shimmer with striking similarities. Montesanti’s […]

The post Memoir Is a Form of Drag appeared first on
hook: Makeup as philosophy: how drag teaches us that identity is always performance, and that's precisely the point.

title: A Diary from the Psychic Capital of the World
description: “The Psychic Capital of the World happens to be an unincorporated community in central Florida called Cassadaga.”
hook: Inside Cassadaga, Florida—a town built by spiritualists where every resident claims to see the future you're walking toward.

title: Submitting to the Beast
description: "A father and son in New Orleans—feasting and flaneuring."
hook: A father and son wander New Orleans eating their way through memory, feasting like flâneurs in a city that feeds hunger differently.

title: Time and Water review – a lament for a disappearing world
description: Through the writings of Icelandic poet Andri Snær Magnason, Sara Dosa's new documentary offers a time capsule preserving Iceland's glaciers and the memories bound to them.
hook: Sara Dosa films Iceland's melting glaciers through a poet's words—preserving not ice but the specific griefs bound to it.

title: Warrior Pose
description: If only imperialism could be worked out through breath work.
hook: Yoga's imperial problem: how breath work became the West's tool for ignoring the systems that suffocate us.

title: “I Shot Andy Warhol” Upends the Myth of the Great Man
description: The radical feminist author of “SCUM Manifesto” and the bygone world of Warhol’s Factory come to life in the 1996 film, now restored to 4K.
hook: The radical feminist who shot Warhol comes alive in this restored film—the Factory's myth collapses under her actual presence.

title: Olivia Rodrigo Has No Chill
description: The pop star’s biggest asset is her ferocity—and her new record doubles down on those big, messy feelings.
hook: Olivia Rodrigo's ferocity isn't teenage angst—it's the sound of someone refusing to perform likability, again and again.
